fr.summary: Un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement multi-agents modélise les attaques de manipulation de routage comme un jeu à somme nulle entre les attaquants injectant de fausses données et les défenseurs détectant les anomalies. L'approche fournit des stratégies de détection optimales qui limitent les impacts du pire cas sur le temps de trajet par rapport aux perturbations adversariales.

de.title: Adversariales Reinforcement Learning zur Erkennung von Angriffen mit falscher Dateneinfügung im Fahrzeugroutung
de.summary: Ein Multi-Agent-Reinforcement-Learning-Framework modelliert Routenmanipulationsangriffe als Nullsummenspiel zwischen Angreifern, die falsche Daten einschleusen, und Verteidigern, die Anomalien erkennen. Der Ansatz bietet optimale Erkennungsstrategien, die die Auswirkungen adversarialer Störungen auf die Fahrtzeit im schlimmsten Fall begrenzen.

es.title: Aprendizaje por refuerzo adversarial para detectar ataques de inyección de datos falsos en enrutamiento vehicular
es.summary: Un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo multiagente modela ataques de manipulación de enrutamiento como un juego de suma cero entre atacantes que inyectan datos falsos y defensores que detectan anomalías. El enfoque proporciona estrategias de detección óptimas que limitan los impactos del peor caso en los tiempos de viaje desde perturbaciones adversariales.

title: GPT4o-Receipt: A Dataset and Human Study for AI-Generated Document Forensics
summary: Analysis of AI-generated versus authentic receipts reveals that while humans better perceive visual artifacts, LLMs outperform them at detecting AI documents by verifying arithmetic errors humans cannot spot. This paradox highlights how forensic signals differ between human and machine perception.
content:
arXiv:2603.11442v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Can humans detect AI-generated financial documents better than machines? We present GPT4o-Receipt, a benchmark of 1,235 receipt images pairing GPT-4o-generated receipts with authentic ones from established datasets, evaluated by five state-of-the-art multimodal LLMs and a 30-annotator crowdsourced perceptual study. Our findings reveal a striking paradox: humans are better at seeing AI artifacts, yet worse at detecting AI documents. Human annotators exhibit the largest visual discrimination gap of any evaluator, yet their binary detection F1 falls well below Claude Sonnet 4 and below Gemini 2.5 Flash. This paradox resolves once the mechanism is understood: the dominant forensic signals in AI-generated receipts are arithmetic errors -- invisible to visual inspection but systematically verifiable by LLMs. Humans cannot perceive that a subtotal is incorrect; LLMs verify it in milliseconds. Beyond the human--LLM comparison, our five-model evaluation reveals dramatic performance disparities and calibration differences that render simple accuracy metrics insufficient for detector selection. GPT4o-Receipt, the evaluation framework, and all results are released publicly to support future research in AI document forensics.

zh.title: GPT4o-Receipt：用于AI生成文件取证的数据集和人类研究
zh.summary: 对AI生成和真实收据的分析表明，虽然人类更好地感知视觉假象，但大型语言模型通过验证人类无法发现的算术错误在检测AI文件方面优于人类。这个悖论突出了取证信号在人类和机器感知之间的差异。

fr.title: GPT4o-Receipt : un ensemble de données et une étude humaine pour la forensique de documents générés par l'IA
fr.summary: L'analyse des reçus générés par l'IA par rapport aux reçus authentiques révèle que si les humains perçoivent mieux les artefacts visuels, les modèles de langage de grande taille surpassent les humains dans la détection de documents générés par l'IA en vérifiant les erreurs arithmétiques que les humains ne peuvent pas détecter. Ce paradoxe met en évidence la façon dont les signaux forensiques diffèrent entre la perception humaine et celle de la machine.

de.title: GPT4o-Receipt: Ein Datensatz und eine Humanstudie für KI-generierte Dokumentenforensik
de.summary: Die Analyse von KI-generierten und authentischen Belegen zeigt, dass Menschen zwar visuelle Artefakte besser wahrnehmen, aber große Sprachmodelle sie bei der Erkennung von KI-Dokumenten übertreffen, indem sie arithmetische Fehler überprüfen, die Menschen nicht erkennen können. Dieses Paradoxon zeigt, wie sich forensische Signale zwischen menschlicher und maschineller Wahrnehmung unterscheiden.

es.title: GPT4o-Receipt: un conjunto de datos y un estudio humano para la forense de documentos generados por IA
es.summary: El análisis de recibos generados por IA frente a recibos auténticos revela que mientras los humanos perciben mejor los artefactos visuales, los modelos de lenguaje grande los superan en la detección de documentos generados por IA al verificar errores aritméticos que los humanos no pueden detectar. Esta paradoja destaca cómo las señales forenses difieren entre la percepción humana y la de la máquina.

title: Verified Multi-Agent Orchestration: A Plan-Execute-Verify-Replan Framework for Complex Query Resolution
summary: VMAO coordinates multiple specialized LLM agents through a verification-driven loop that decomposes complex queries into parallel sub-tasks and adaptively replans based on completeness checks. The framework improved answer quality metrics substantially compared to single-agent baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.11445v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present Verified Multi-Agent Orchestration (VMAO), a framework that coordinates specialized LLM-based agents through a verification-driven iterative loop. Given a complex query, our system decomposes it into a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of sub-questions, executes them through domain-specific agents in parallel, verifies result completeness via LLM-based evaluation, and adaptively replans to address gaps. The key contributions are: (1) dependency-aware parallel execution over a DAG of sub-questions with automatic context propagation, (2) verification-driven adaptive replanning that uses an LLM-based verifier as an orchestration-level coordination signal, and (3) configurable stop conditions that balance answer quality against resource usage. On 25 expert-curated market research queries, VMAO improves answer completeness from 3.1 to 4.2 and source quality from 2.6 to 4.1 (1-5 scale) compared to a single-agent baseline, demonstrating that orchestration-level verification is an effective mechanism for multi-agent quality assurance.

zh.title: 经过验证的多智能体编排：用于复杂查询解决的计划-执行-验证-重新规划框架
zh.summary: VMAO通过验证驱动的循环协调多个专门的大型语言模型智能体，该循环将复杂查询分解为并行子任务，并根据完整性检查自适应地重新规划。与单智能体基线相比，该框架大幅改进了答案质量指标。

fr.title: Orchestration multi-agents vérifiée : un cadre Plan-Exécution-Vérification-Replanification pour la résolution de requêtes complexes
fr.summary: VMAO coordonne plusieurs agents LLM spécialisés via une boucle axée sur la vérification qui décompose les requêtes complexes en sous-tâches parallèles et replans de manière adaptative en fonction des contrôles de complétude. Le cadre a amélioré substantiellement les métriques de qualité des réponses par rapport aux lignes de base mono-agents.

de.title: Verifizierte Multi-Agent-Orchestrierung: Ein Plan-Execute-Verify-Replan-Rahmen für die Auflösung komplexer Abfragen
de.summary: VMAO koordiniert mehrere spezialisierte LLM-Agenten durch eine verifikationsgesteuerte Schleife, die komplexe Abfragen in parallele Teilaufgaben zerlegt und adaptiv neu plant, basierend auf Vollständigkeitsprüfungen. Das Framework verbesserte Antwortqualitätsmetriken erheblich im Vergleich zu Single-Agent-Baselines.

es.title: Orquestación Multi-Agente Verificada: Un Marco Plan-Ejecutar-Verificar-Replanificar para la Resolución de Consultas Complejas
es.summary: VMAO coordina múltiples agentes LLM especializados a través de un bucle impulsado por verificación que descompone consultas complejas en subtareas paralelas y replanes adaptativamente basándose en comprobaciones de integridad. El marco mejoró sustancialmente las métricas de calidad de respuesta en comparación con líneas de base de agente único.

title: Examining Users' Behavioural Intention to Use OpenClaw Through the Cognition--Affect--Conation Framework
summary: A survey of 436 OpenClaw users shows that positive perceptions (personalization, intelligence) strengthen adoption intent while negative perceptions (privacy concerns, opacity) increase distrust. The findings illuminate psychological mechanisms influencing autonomous AI agent adoption.
content:
arXiv:2603.11455v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This study examines users' behavioural intention to use OpenClaw through the Cognition--Affect--Conation (CAC) framework. The research investigates how cognitive perceptions of the system influence affective responses and subsequently shape behavioural intention. Enabling factors include perceived personalisation, perceived intelligence, and relative advantage, while inhibiting factors include privacy concern, algorithmic opacity, and perceived risk. Survey data from 436 OpenClaw users were analysed using structural equation modelling. The results show that positive perceptions strengthen users' attitudes toward OpenClaw, which increase behavioural intention, whereas negative perceptions increase distrust and reduce intention to use the system. The study provides insights into the psychological mechanisms influencing the adoption of autonomous AI agents.

zh.title: 通过认知-情感-意向框架审视用户使用OpenClaw的行为意图
zh.summary: 对436名OpenClaw用户的调查显示，积极感知（个性化、智能性）增强了采用意图，而消极感知（隐私顾虑、不透明性）增加了不信任。该研究阐明了影响自主AI代理采用的心理机制。

fr.title: Examen de l'intention comportementale des utilisateurs d'utiliser OpenClaw à travers le cadre Cognition-Affect-Conation
fr.summary: Un sondage auprès de 436 utilisateurs d'OpenClaw montre que les perceptions positives (personnalisation, intelligence) renforcent l'intention d'adoption, tandis que les perceptions négatives (préoccupations concernant la confidentialité, opacité) augmentent la méfiance. Les conclusions éclairent les mécanismes psychologiques influençant l'adoption d'agents d'IA autonomes.

de.title: Untersuchung der Verhaltensabsicht von Benutzern, OpenClaw zu nutzen, durch das Cognition-Affect-Conation-Framework
de.summary: Eine Umfrage unter 436 OpenClaw-Benutzern zeigt, dass positive Wahrnehmungen (Personalisierung, Intelligenz) die Adoptionsabsicht verstärken, während negative Wahrnehmungen (Datenschutzbedenken, Undurchsichtigkeit) das Misstrauen erhöhen. Die Erkenntnisse verdeutlichen die psychologischen Mechanismen, die die Übernahme autonomer KI-Agenten beeinflussen.

es.title: Examinando la Intención Conductual de los Usuarios de Usar OpenClaw a Través del Marco Cognición-Afecto-Conación
es.summary: Una encuesta de 436 usuarios de OpenClaw muestra que las percepciones positivas (personalización, inteligencia) fortalecen la intención de adopción, mientras que las percepciones negativas (preocupaciones de privacidad, opacidad) aumentan la desconfianza. Los hallazgos iluminan los mecanismos psicológicos que influyen en la adopción de agentes de IA autónomos.

title: Multi-Agent Collaboration for Automated Design Exploration on High Performance Computing Systems
summary: MADA orchestrates multiple LLM agents to automate complex scientific design workflows on high-performance computing systems. Demonstrated on Inertial Confinement Fusion challenges, the system iteratively refines designs with minimal manual intervention.
content:
arXiv:2603.11515v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Today's scientific challenges, from climate modeling to Inertial Confinement Fusion design to novel material design, require exploring huge design spaces. In order to enable high-impact scientific discovery, we need to scale up our ability to test hypotheses, generate results, and learn from them rapidly. We present MADA (Multi-Agent Design Assistant), a Large Language Model (LLM) powered multi-agent framework that coordinates specialized agents for complex design workflows. A Job Management Agent (JMA) launches and manages ensemble simulations on HPC systems, a Geometry Agent (GA) generates meshes, and an Inverse Design Agent (IDA) proposes new designs informed by simulation outcomes. While general purpose, we focus development and validation on Richtmyer--Meshkov Instability (RMI) suppression, a critical challenge in Inertial Confinement Fusion. We evaluate on two complementary settings: running a hydrodynamics simulations on HPC systems, and using a pre-trained machine learning surrogate for rapid design exploration. Our results demonstrate that the MADA system successfully executes iterative design refinement, automatically improving designs toward optimal RMI suppression with minimal manual intervention. Our framework reduces cumbersome manual workflow setup, and enables automated design exploration at scale. More broadly, it demonstrates a reusable pattern for coupling reasoning, simulation, specialized tools, and coordinated workflows to accelerate scientific discovery.

zh.title: 高性能计算系统上自动化设计探索的多代理协作
zh.summary: MADA协调多个大语言模型代理以在高性能计算系统上自动化复杂的科学设计工作流。在惯性约束聚变挑战上展示，该系统以最少的手动干预迭代优化设计。

fr.title: Collaboration Multi-Agent pour l'Exploration Automatisée de la Conception sur les Systèmes Informatiques Haute Performance
fr.summary: MADA orchestre plusieurs agents LLM pour automatiser les flux de travail de conception scientifique complexes sur les systèmes informatiques haute performance. Démontré sur les défis de la fusion par confinement inertiel, le système affine itérativement les conceptions avec une intervention manuelle minimale.

de.title: Multi-Agent-Zusammenarbeit für automatisierte Designerkundung auf Hochleistungsrechensystemen
de.summary: MADA orchestriert mehrere LLM-Agenten, um komplexe wissenschaftliche Designworkflows auf Hochleistungsrechensystemen zu automatisieren. Das System wurde bei Herausforderungen der Trägheitseinschluss-Fusionierung demonstriert und verfeinert iterativ Designs mit minimalem manuellem Eingriff.

es.title: Colaboración Multi-Agente para la Exploración Automatizada de Diseño en Sistemas de Computación de Alto Rendimiento
es.summary: MADA orquesta múltiples agentes LLM para automatizar flujos de trabajo de diseño científico complejo en sistemas de computación de alto rendimiento. Demostrado en desafíos de Fusión por Confinamiento Inercial, el sistema refina iterativamente los diseños con intervención manual mínima.

title: Expert Threshold Routing for Autoregressive Language Modeling with Dynamic Computation Allocation and Load Balancing
summary: Expert Threshold (ET) routing improves Mixture-of-Experts models by enabling dynamic computation allocation without auxiliary load-balancing losses. The method achieved 1.6× better efficiency than token-choice MoE during pretraining at 2.4B parameters.
content:
arXiv:2603.11535v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Token-choice Mixture-of-Experts (TC-MoE) routes each token to a fixed number of experts, limiting dynamic computation allocation and requiring auxiliary losses to maintain load balance. We propose Expert Threshold (ET) routing, where each expert maintains an exponential moving average (EMA) threshold estimated from the global token distribution. At both training and inference, each token is independently routed to an expert if its score exceeds the expert's threshold, enabling dynamic computation allocation while achieving load balance without auxiliary losses. This fully causal mechanism eliminates dependence on other tokens in the batch, making it well-suited for autoregressive language modeling. In pretraining experiments scaling to 2.4B parameters on FineWeb-Edu, ET achieves 0.067 lower cross-entropy loss than TC-MoE, equivalent to reaching the same performance with 1.6$\times$ fewer tokens.

zh.title: 自回归语言建模的专家阈值路由：动态计算分配和负载均衡
zh.summary: 专家阈值（ET）路由通过在无需辅助负载均衡损失的情况下实现动态计算分配来改进混合专家模型。该方法在2.4B参数预训练中相比令牌选择MoE实现了1.6倍的效率提升。

fr.title: Routage par Seuil d'Expert pour la Modélisation du Langage Autorégressif avec Allocation Dynamique du Calcul et Équilibrage de Charge
fr.summary: Le routage par Seuil d'Expert (ET) améliore les modèles Mixture-of-Experts en permettant une allocation dynamique du calcul sans pertes d'équilibrage de charge auxiliaires. La méthode a atteint une efficacité 1,6× supérieure à celle du MoE à choix de jetons lors du préentraînement à 2,4B paramètres.

de.title: Expert-Schwellen-Routing für autoregressives Sprachmodellieren mit dynamischer Rechnerallokation und Lastausgleich
de.summary: Expert-Schwellen (ET)-Routing verbessert Mixture-of-Experts-Modelle durch dynamische Rechnerallokation ohne zusätzliche Lastausgleichsverluste. Das Verfahren erreichte bei 2,4B-Parametern-Vortraining eine 1,6× bessere Effizienz als Token-Choice-MoE.

es.title: Enrutamiento de Umbral de Experto para Modelado de Lenguaje Autorregresivo con Asignación de Cálculo Dinámico y Equilibrio de Carga
es.summary: El enrutamiento de Umbral de Experto (ET) mejora los modelos Mixture-of-Experts al permitir asignación dinámica de cálculo sin pérdidas de equilibrio de carga auxiliares. El método logró 1,6× mejor eficiencia que MoE de elección de tokens durante el preentrenamiento en 2,4B parámetros.

title: AI Knows What's Wrong But Cannot Fix It: Helicoid Dynamics in Frontier LLMs Under High-Stakes Decisions
summary: A case series documents "helicoid dynamics"—a failure mode observed across seven leading LLMs where they correctly identify errors but then reproduce the same mistakes at higher sophistication levels. The pattern is particularly pronounced in high-stakes scenarios where rigorous decision-making and comfort diverge.
content:
arXiv:2603.11559v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models perform reliably when their outputs can be checked: solving equations, writing code, retrieving facts. They perform differently when checking is impossible, as when a clinician chooses an irreversible treatment on incomplete data, or an investor commits capital under fundamental uncertainty.
  Helicoid dynamics is the name given to a specific failure regime in that second domain: a system engages competently, drifts into error, accurately names what went wrong, then reproduces the same pattern at a higher level of sophistication, recognizing it is looping and continuing nonetheless. This prospective case series documents that regime across seven leading systems (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Perplexity, Llama families), tested across clinical diagnosis, investment evaluation, and high-consequence interview scenarios. Despite explicit protocols designed to sustain rigorous partnership, all exhibited the pattern. When confronted with it, they attributed its persistence to structural factors in their training, beyond what conversation can reach.
  Under high stakes, when being rigorous and being comfortable diverge, these systems tend toward comfort, becoming less reliable precisely when reliability matters most. Twelve testable hypotheses are proposed, with implications for agentic AI oversight and human-AI collaboration.
  The helicoid is tractable. Identifying it, naming it, and understanding its boundary conditions are the necessary first steps toward LLMs that remain trustworthy partners precisely when the decisions are hardest and the stakes are highest.

zh.title: 人工智能知道出了什么问题但无法修复：前沿大型语言模型中高风险决策下的螺旋动态
zh.summary: 一项案例系列记录了"螺旋动态"——在七个领先的大型语言模型中观察到的失败模式，它们正确识别错误，但在更高的复杂性水平上重复相同的错误。这种模式在严谨决策和舒适度不同的高风险场景中特别明显。

fr.title: L'IA sait ce qui ne va pas mais ne peut pas le réparer : Dynamiques hélicoïdales dans les LLM de pointe sous des décisions à enjeux élevés
fr.summary: Une série de cas documente les « dynamiques hélicoïdales » — un mode de défaillance observé dans sept LLM de premier plan où ils identifient correctement les erreurs mais reproduisent ensuite les mêmes erreurs à des niveaux de sophistication plus élevés. Le schéma est particulièrement prononcé dans les scénarios à enjeux élevés où la prise de décision rigoureuse et le confort divergent.

de.title: KI kennt das Problem, kann es aber nicht beheben: Helikale Dynamik in modernen LLMs unter hochriskanten Entscheidungen
de.summary: Eine Fallserie dokumentiert „helikale Dynamik" – ein Fehlermodus, der bei sieben führenden LLMs beobachtet wurde, bei dem sie Fehler korrekt erkennen, dann aber auf höheren Komplexitätsstufen dieselben Fehler wiederholen. Das Muster ist besonders in hochriskanten Szenarien ausgeprägt, in denen rigorose Entscheidungsfindung und Komfort auseinandergehen.

es.title: La IA sabe qué está mal pero no puede arreglarlo: Dinámicas helicoidales en LLM de frontera bajo decisiones de alto riesgo
es.summary: Una serie de casos documenta la "dinámica helicoidal", un modo de fallo observado en siete LLM líderes donde identifican correctamente los errores pero luego reproducen los mismos errores a niveles de sofisticación más altos. El patrón es particularmente pronunciado en escenarios de alto riesgo donde la toma de decisiones rigurosa y la comodidad divergen.

title: Leveraging Large Language Models and Survival Analysis for Early Prediction of Chemotherapy Outcomes
summary: Researchers combine LLM-based clinical data extraction with survival analysis to predict chemotherapy outcomes for breast cancer patients, achieving 73% accuracy. The approach addresses data sparsity challenges and enables personalized treatment planning.
content:
arXiv:2603.11594v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Chemotherapy for cancer treatment is costly and accompanied by severe side effects, highlighting the critical need for early prediction of treatment outcomes to improve patient management and informed decision-making. Predictive models for chemotherapy outcomes using real-world data face challenges, including the absence of explicit phenotypes and treatment outcome labels such as cancer progression and toxicity. This study addresses these challenges by employing Large Language Models (LLMs) and ontology-based techniques for phenotypes and outcome label extraction from patient notes. We focused on one of the most frequently occurring cancers, breast cancer, due to its high prevalence and significant variability in patient response to treatment, making it a critical area for improving predictive modeling. The dataset included features such as vitals, demographics, staging, biomarkers, and performance scales. Drug regimens and their combinations were extracted from the chemotherapy plans in the EMR data and shortlisted based on NCCN guidelines, verified with NIH standards, and analyzed through survival modeling. The proposed approach significantly reduced phenotypes sparsity and improved predictive accuracy. Random Survival Forest was used to predict time-to-failure, achieving a C-index of 73%, and utilized as a classifier at a specific time point to predict treatment outcomes, with accuracy and F1 scores above 70%. The outcome probabilities were validated for reliability by calibration curves. We extended our approach to four other cancer types. This research highlights the potential of early prediction of treatment outcomes using LLM-based clinical data extraction enabling personalized treatment plans with better patient outcomes.

zh.title: 利用大型语言模型和生存分析提前预测化疗结果
zh.summary: 研究人员将基于大型语言模型的临床数据提取与生存分析相结合，以预测乳腺癌患者的化疗结果，准确率达到73%。该方法解决了数据稀疏的挑战，并实现了个性化治疗规划。

fr.title: Exploiter les grands modèles de langage et l'analyse de survie pour la prédiction précoce des résultats de la chimiothérapie
fr.summary: Les chercheurs combinent l'extraction de données cliniques basée sur les LLM avec l'analyse de survie pour prédire les résultats de la chimiothérapie chez les patients atteints d'un cancer du sein, atteignant une précision de 73%. L'approche résout les défis de la rareté des données et permet une planification thérapeutique personnalisée.

de.title: Nutzung von großen Sprachmodellen und Überlebensanalyse zur Frühvorhersage von Chemotherapieergebnissen
de.summary: Forscher kombinieren LLM-gestützte klinische Datenextraktion mit Überlebensanalyse, um Chemotherapieergebnisse bei Brustkrebspatienten vorherzusagen und erreichen eine Genauigkeit von 73%. Der Ansatz bewältigt die Herausforderungen der Dateneinfachheit und ermöglicht eine personalisierte Behandlungsplanung.

es.title: Aprovechamiento de grandes modelos de lenguaje y análisis de supervivencia para la predicción temprana de resultados de quimioterapia
es.summary: Los investigadores combinan la extracción de datos clínicos basada en LLM con análisis de supervivencia para predecir los resultados de la quimioterapia en pacientes con cáncer de mama, logrando una precisión del 73%. El enfoque aborda los desafíos de escasez de datos y permite la planificación del tratamiento personalizado.

title: See, Symbolize, Act: Grounding VLMs with Spatial Representations for Better Gameplay
summary: Vision-Language Models improve performance in interactive environments when provided with accurate symbolic scene representations alongside visual frames. However, performance degrades when VLMs must extract symbols themselves, indicating perception quality as a critical bottleneck.
content:
arXiv:2603.11601v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) excel at describing visual scenes, yet struggle to translate perception into precise, grounded actions. We investigate whether providing VLMs with both the visual frame and the symbolic representation of the scene can improve their performance in interactive environments. We evaluate three state-of-the-art VLMs across Atari games, VizDoom, and AI2-THOR, comparing frame-only, frame with self-extracted symbols, frame with ground-truth symbols, and symbol-only pipelines. Our results indicate that all models benefit when the symbolic information is accurate. However, when VLMs extract symbols themselves, performance becomes dependent on model capability and scene complexity. We further investigate how accurately VLMs can extract symbolic information from visual inputs and how noise in these symbols affects decision-making and gameplay performance. Our findings reveal that symbolic grounding is beneficial in VLMs only when symbol extraction is reliable, and highlight perception quality as a central bottleneck for future VLM-based agents.

zh.title: 看、符号化、行动：用空间表示来接地视觉语言模型以实现更好的游戏性能
zh.summary: 当视觉语言模型配备准确的符号场景表示以及视觉帧时，它们在交互环境中的性能会得到改善。但是，当VLM必须自己提取符号时，性能会下降，这表明感知质量是关键瓶颈。

fr.title: Voir, symboliser, agir : Ancrer les VLM avec des représentations spatiales pour un meilleur gameplay
fr.summary: Les modèles vision-langage améliorent les performances dans les environnements interactifs lorsqu'ils sont fournis avec des représentations symboliques de scènes précises aux côtés des images. Cependant, les performances se dégradent lorsque les VLM doivent extraire les symboles eux-mêmes, indiquant que la qualité de la perception est un goulot d'étranglement critique.

de.title: Sehen, symbolisieren, handeln: VLMs mit räumlichen Darstellungen für besseres Gameplay verankern
de.summary: Vision-Language-Modelle verbessern die Leistung in interaktiven Umgebungen, wenn sie mit genauen symbolischen Szenendarstellungen neben visuellen Rahmen versehen werden. Die Leistung verschlechtert sich jedoch, wenn VLMs Symbole selbst extrahieren müssen, was darauf hinweist, dass die Wahrnehmungsqualität ein kritischer Engpass ist.

es.title: Ver, simbolizar, actuar: Anclar VLM con representaciones espaciales para un mejor gameplay
es.summary: Los modelos de visión y lenguaje mejoran el rendimiento en entornos interactivos cuando se les proporcionan representaciones simbólicas de escenas precisas junto con fotogramas visuales. Sin embargo, el rendimiento se degrada cuando los VLM deben extraer símbolos por sí solos, lo que indica que la calidad de la percepción es un cuello de botella crítico.

title: The Density of Cross-Persistence Diagrams and Its Applications
summary: Researchers present the first systematic study of cross-persistence diagrams, a topological data analysis tool that captures relationships between topological features of paired point clouds. The work establishes theoretical foundations and a machine learning framework that outperforms existing techniques in density prediction and point cloud classification.
content:
arXiv:2603.11623v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Topological Data Analysis (TDA) provides powerful tools to explore the shape and structure of data through topological features such as clusters, loops, and voids. Persistence diagrams are a cornerstone of TDA, capturing the evolution of these features across scales. While effective for analyzing individual manifolds, persistence diagrams do not account for interactions between pairs of them. Cross-persistence diagrams (cross-barcodes), introduced recently, address this limitation by characterizing relationships between topological features of two point clouds. In this work, we present the first systematic study of the density of cross-persistence diagrams. We prove its existence, establish theoretical foundations for its statistical use, and design the first machine learning framework for predicting cross-persistence density directly from point cloud coordinates and distance matrices. Our statistical approach enables the distinction of point clouds sampled from different manifolds by leveraging the linear characteristics of cross-persistence diagrams. Interestingly, we find that introducing noise can enhance our ability to distinguish point clouds, uncovering its novel utility in TDA applications. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our methods through experiments on diverse datasets, where our approach consistently outperforms existing techniques in density prediction and achieves superior results in point cloud distinction tasks. Our findings contribute to a broader understanding of cross-persistence diagrams and open new avenues for their application in data analysis, including potential insights into time-series domain tasks and the geometry of AI-generated texts. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/Verdangeta/TDA_experiments

zh.title: 交叉持久图的密度及其应用
zh.summary: 研究人员首次系统地研究了交叉持久图，这是一种拓扑数据分析工具，捕捉成对点云拓扑特征之间的关系。这项工作建立了理论基础和机器学习框架，在密度预测和点云分类中的表现超过了现有技术。

fr.title: La Densité des Diagrammes de Persistance Croisée et Ses Applications
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent la première étude systématique des diagrammes de persistance croisée, un outil d'analyse de données topologiques qui capture les relations entre les caractéristiques topologiques des nuages de points appariés. Le travail établit des fondations théoriques et un cadre d'apprentissage automatique qui surpasse les techniques existantes dans la prédiction de densité et la classification des nuages de points.

de.title: Die Dichte von Kreuzpersistenzdiagrammen und ihre Anwendungen
de.summary: Forscher präsentieren die erste systematische Studie von Kreuzpersistenzdiagrammen, ein topologisches Datenanalysetool, das Beziehungen zwischen topologischen Merkmalen gepaarter Punktwolken erfasst. Die Arbeit etabliert theoretische Grundlagen und ein maschinelles Lernframework, das bestehende Techniken in der Dichtevorhersage und Klassifikation von Punktwolken übertrifft.

es.title: La Densidad de Diagramas de Persistencia Cruzada y Sus Aplicaciones
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan el primer estudio sistemático de diagramas de persistencia cruzada, una herramienta de análisis de datos topológicos que captura relaciones entre características topológicas de nubes de puntos emparejadas. El trabajo establece fundamentos teóricos y un marco de aprendizaje automático que supera las técnicas existentes en predicción de densidad y clasificación de nubes de puntos.

title: VisDoT : Enhancing Visual Reasoning through Human-Like Interpretation Grounding and Decomposition of Thought
summary: VisDoT is a framework that improves vision-language models' ability to understand charts and complex visual content by mimicking human perceptual processes. The approach achieves significant improvements on multiple benchmarks, including surpassing GPT-4o on advanced chart understanding tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.11631v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) struggle to reliably detect visual primitives in charts and align them with semantic representations, which severely limits their performance on complex visual reasoning. This lack of perceptual grounding constitutes a major bottleneck for chart-based reasoning. We propose VisDoT, a framework that enhances visual reasoning through human-like interpretation grounding. We formalize four perceptual tasks based on the theory of graphical perception, including position and length. Building on this foundation, we introduce Decomposition-of-Thought (DoT) prompting, which sequentially separates questions into visual perception sub-questions and logic sub-questions. Fine-tuning InternVL with VisDoT achieves a +11.2% improvement on ChartQA and surpasses GPT-4o on the more challenging ChartQAPro benchmark. On the newly introduced VisDoTQA benchmark, the model improves by +33.2%. Furthermore, consistent zero-shot gains on diverse open-domain VQA benchmarks confirm the generalizability of the perception-logic separation strategy for visual question answering. VisDoT leverages human-like perception to enhance visual grounding, achieving state-of-the-art chart understanding and interpretable visual reasoning.

zh.title: VisDoT：通过类人解释基础和思维分解增强视觉推理
zh.summary: VisDoT是一个框架，通过模拟人类感知过程来改进视觉语言模型理解图表和复杂视觉内容的能力。该方法在多个基准上实现了显著改进，包括在高级图表理解任务中超越GPT-4o。

fr.title: VisDoT : Améliorer le raisonnement visuel par l'interprétation semblable à l'homme, l'ancrage et la décomposition de la pensée
fr.summary: VisDoT est un framework qui améliore la capacité des modèles de vision-langage à comprendre les graphiques et le contenu visuel complexe en imitant les processus de perception humaine. L'approche réalise des améliorations significatives sur plusieurs benchmarks, y compris le dépassement de GPT-4o sur les tâches avancées de compréhension de graphiques.

de.title: VisDoT: Verbesserung des visuellen Denkens durch menschenähnliche Interpretationsgrundlage und Gedankenzerlegung
de.summary: VisDoT ist ein Framework, das die Fähigkeit von Vision-Language-Modellen, Diagramme und komplexe visuelle Inhalte zu verstehen, durch Nachahmung menschlicher Wahrnehmungsprozesse verbessert. Der Ansatz erreicht erhebliche Verbesserungen bei mehreren Benchmarks, einschließlich der Überwindung von GPT-4o bei fortgeschrittenen Aufgaben zum Verständnis von Diagrammen.

es.title: VisDoT: Mejora del razonamiento visual a través de la interpretación similar a la humana, fundamentación y descomposición del pensamiento
es.summary: VisDoT es un framework que mejora la capacidad de los modelos de visión-lenguaje para comprender gráficos y contenido visual complejo al imitar los procesos de percepción humana. El enfoque logra mejoras significativas en múltiples benchmarks, incluida la superación de GPT-4o en tareas avanzadas de comprensión de gráficos.

title: LLMs can construct powerful representations and streamline sample-efficient supervised learning
summary: Large language models can automatically generate structured rubrics for feature engineering, significantly improving supervised learning on complex, multimodal datasets. This approach outperforms traditional methods on healthcare tasks while remaining cost-effective and auditable.
content:
arXiv:2603.11679v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As real-world datasets become increasingly complex and heterogeneous, supervised learning is often bottlenecked by input representation design. Modeling multimodal data for downstream tasks, such as time-series, free text, and structured records, often requires non-trivial domain-specific engineering. We propose an agentic pipeline to streamline this process. First, an LLM analyzes a small but diverse subset of text-serialized input examples in-context to synthesize a global rubric, which acts as a programmatic specification for extracting and organizing evidence. This rubric is then used to transform naive text-serializations of inputs into a more standardized format for downstream models. We also describe local rubrics, which are task-conditioned summaries generated by an LLM. Across 15 clinical tasks from the EHRSHOT benchmark, our rubric-based approaches significantly outperform traditional count-feature models, naive text-serialization-based LLM baselines, and a clinical foundation model, which is pretrained on orders of magnitude more data. Beyond performance, rubrics offer several advantages for operational healthcare settings such as being easy to audit, cost-effectiveness to deploy at scale, and they can be converted to tabular representations that unlock a swath of machine learning techniques.

zh.title: 大语言模型能够构建强大的表示并简化样本高效的监督学习
zh.summary: 大语言模型可以自动为特征工程生成结构化的评分标准，显著改进复杂、多模态数据集的监督学习。这种方法在医疗保健任务上的表现优于传统方法，同时保持成本效益和可审计性。

fr.title: Les LLMs peuvent construire des représentations puissantes et rationaliser l'apprentissage supervisé efficace en échantillons
fr.summary: Les grands modèles de langage peuvent générer automatiquement des rubriques structurées pour l'ingénierie des caractéristiques, améliorant considérablement l'apprentissage supervisé sur les ensembles de données complexes et multimodaux. Cette approche surpasse les méthodes traditionnelles sur les tâches de santé tout en restant rentable et vérifiable.

de.title: LLMs können mächtige Darstellungen aufbauen und probeneffizientes überwachtes Lernen rationalisieren
de.summary: Große Sprachmodelle können automatisch strukturierte Rubriken für Feature Engineering generieren und das überwachte Lernen auf komplexen, multimodalen Datensätzen erheblich verbessern. Dieser Ansatz übertrifft traditionelle Methoden bei Aufgaben im Gesundheitswesen, während er kostengünstig und überprüfbar bleibt.

es.title: Los LLMs pueden construir representaciones poderosas y simplificar el aprendizaje supervisado eficiente en muestras
es.summary: Los grandes modelos de lenguaje pueden generar automáticamente rúbricas estructuradas para la ingeniería de características, mejorando significativamente el aprendizaje supervisado en conjuntos de datos complejos y multimodales. Este enfoque supera a los métodos tradicionales en tareas de atención médica mientras se mantiene rentable y auditable.

title: Explicit Logic Channel for Validation and Enhancement of MLLMs on Zero-Shot Tasks
summary: An Explicit Logic Channel framework validates and enhances multimodal large language models for zero-shot tasks by running explicit logical reasoning in parallel with the model's implicit reasoning. The method improves performance while providing explainability and trustworthiness for vision-language comprehension tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.11689v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Frontier Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) exhibit remarkable capabilities in Visual-Language Comprehension (VLC) tasks. However, they are often deployed as zero-shot solution to new tasks in a black-box manner. Validating and understanding the behavior of these models become important for application to new task. We propose an Explicit Logic Channel, in parallel with the black-box model channel, to perform explicit logical reasoning for model validation, selection and enhancement. The frontier MLLM, encapsulating latent vision-language knowledge, can be considered as an Implicit Logic Channel. The proposed Explicit Logic Channel, mimicking human logical reasoning, incorporates a LLM, a VFM, and logical reasoning with probabilistic inference for factual, counterfactual, and relational reasoning over the explicit visual evidence. A Consistency Rate (CR) is proposed for cross-channel validation and model selection, even without ground-truth annotations. Additionally, cross-channel integration further improves performance in zero-shot tasks over MLLMs, grounded with explicit visual evidence to enhance trustworthiness. Comprehensive experiments conducted for two representative VLC tasks, i.e., MC-VQA and HC-REC, on three challenging benchmarks, with 11 recent open-source MLLMs from 4 frontier families. Our systematic evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of proposed ELC and CR for model validation, selection and improvement on MLLMs with enhanced explainability and trustworthiness.

zh.title: 用于多模态大语言模型零样本任务验证和增强的显式逻辑通道
zh.summary: 显式逻辑通道框架通过将显式逻辑推理与模型的隐式推理并行运行来验证和增强多模态大语言模型的零样本任务性能。该方法改进性能，同时为视觉语言理解任务提供可解释性和可信度。

fr.title: Canal de logique explicite pour la validation et l'amélioration des MLLMs sur les tâches zero-shot
fr.summary: Un framework de canal de logique explicite valide et améliore les modèles de langage multimodaux volumineux pour les tâches zero-shot en exécutant un raisonnement logique explicite en parallèle avec le raisonnement implicite du modèle. La méthode améliore les performances tout en fournissant une explicabilité et une fiabilité pour les tâches de compréhension vision-langage.

de.title: Expliziter Logikkanal zur Validierung und Verbesserung von MLLMs bei Zero-Shot-Aufgaben
de.summary: Ein Framework für explizite Logikkanäle validiert und verbessert multimodale große Sprachmodelle für Zero-Shot-Aufgaben, indem es explizites logisches Denken parallel zum impliziten Denken des Modells ausführt. Die Methode verbessert die Leistung und bietet Erklärbarkeit und Vertrauenswürdigkeit für Vision-Language-Verständnisaufgaben.

es.title: Canal de lógica explícita para validación y mejora de MLLMs en tareas zero-shot
es.summary: Un framework de canal de lógica explícita valida y mejora modelos de lenguaje multimodales grandes para tareas zero-shot ejecutando razonamiento lógico explícito en paralelo con el razonamiento implícito del modelo. El método mejora el rendimiento mientras proporciona explicabilidad y confiabilidad para tareas de comprensión visión-lenguaje.

title: STAIRS-Former: Spatio-Temporal Attention with Interleaved Recursive Structure Transformer for Offline Multi-task Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
summary: STAIRS-Former is a transformer architecture designed for multi-agent reinforcement learning that improves inter-agent coordination and captures long-horizon temporal dependencies. The approach achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse multi-agent benchmarks and multi-task datasets.
content:
arXiv:2603.11691v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Offline multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) with multi-task datasets is challenging due to varying numbers of agents across tasks and the need to generalize to unseen scenarios. Prior works employ transformers with observation tokenization and hierarchical skill learning to address these issues. However, they underutilize the transformer attention mechanism for inter-agent coordination and rely on a single history token, which limits their ability to capture long-horizon temporal dependencies in partially observable MARL settings. In this paper, we propose STAIRS-Former, a transformer architecture augmented with spatial and temporal hierarchies that enables effective attention over critical tokens while capturing long interaction histories. We further introduce token dropout to enhance robustness and generalization across varying agent populations. Extensive experiments on diverse multi-agent benchmarks, including SMAC, SMAC-v2, MPE, and MaMuJoCo, with multi-task datasets demonstrate that STAIRS-Former consistently outperforms prior methods and achieves new state-of-the-art performance.

zh.title: STAIRS-Former: 用于离线多任务多智能体强化学习的时空注意力交错递归结构变换器
zh.summary: STAIRS-Former是为多智能体强化学习设计的变换器架构，改进了智能体间协调并捕捉长期时间依赖关系。该方法在多种多智能体基准和多任务数据集上实现了最先进的性能。

fr.title: STAIRS-Former: Transformateur de Structure Récursive Entrelacée avec Attention Spatio-Temporelle pour l'Apprentissage par Renforcement Multi-Agent Multi-Tâche Hors Ligne
fr.summary: STAIRS-Former est une architecture de transformateur conçue pour l'apprentissage par renforcement multi-agent qui améliore la coordination entre les agents et capture les dépendances temporelles à long terme. L'approche atteint les performances de pointe sur les benchmarks multi-agents diversifiés et les ensembles de données multi-tâches.

de.title: STAIRS-Former: Raumzeitlicher Aufmerksamkeitstransformator mit verschachtelter rekursiver Struktur für Offline-Multi-Task-Multi-Agent-Verstärkungslernens
de.summary: STAIRS-Former ist eine Transformator-Architektur für Multi-Agent-Verstärkungslernens, die die Koordination zwischen Agenten verbessert und langfristige zeitliche Abhängigkeiten erfasst. Der Ansatz erreicht moderne Leistungen bei verschiedenen Multi-Agent-Benchmarks und Multi-Task-Datensätzen.

es.title: STAIRS-Former: Transformador de Atención Espacio-Temporal con Estructura Recursiva Intercalada para Aprendizaje por Refuerzo Multi-Agente Multi-Tarea Offline
es.summary: STAIRS-Former es una arquitectura de transformador diseñada para aprendizaje por refuerzo multi-agente que mejora la coordinación entre agentes y captura dependencias temporales a largo plazo. El enfoque logra un rendimiento de última generación en diversos puntos de referencia multi-agente y conjuntos de datos multi-tarea.

title: Scaling Laws for Educational AI Agents
summary: Researchers propose that educational AI agent performance scales through structured dimensions beyond model size, including role clarity, skill depth, and tool completeness. The framework demonstrates effectiveness through 330+ educational agent profiles serving K-12 subjects with 1,100+ skill modules.
content:
arXiv:2603.11709v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While scaling laws for Large Language Models (LLMs) have been extensively studied along dimensions of model parameters, training data, and compute, the scaling behavior of LLM-based educational agents remains unexplored. We propose that educational agent capability scales not merely with the underlying model size, but through structured dimensions that we collectively term the Agent Scaling Law: role definition clarity, skill depth, tool completeness, runtime capability, and educator expertise injection. Central to this framework is AgentProfile, a structured JSON-based specification that serves as the mechanism enabling systematic capability growth of educational agents. We present EduClaw, a profile-driven multi-agent platform that operationalizes this scaling law, demonstrating its effectiveness through the construction and deployment of 330+ educational agent profiles encompassing 1,100+ skill modules across K-12 subjects. Our empirical observations suggest that educational agent performance scales predictably with profile structural richness. We identify two complementary scaling axes -- Tool Scaling and Skill Scaling -- as future directions, arguing that the path to more capable educational AI lies not solely in larger models, but in stronger structured capability systems.

zh.title: 教育AI代理的扩展法则
zh.summary: 研究人员提出教育AI代理的性能通过包括角色清晰度、技能深度和工具完整性等结构维度而不仅仅是模型大小来扩展。该框架通过330多个教育代理配置文件为K-12科目提供1,100多个技能模块的有效性进行了演示。

fr.title: Lois de Mise à l'Échelle pour les Agents IA Éducatifs
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent que les performances des agents IA éducatifs s'étendent par des dimensions structurées au-delà de la taille du modèle, y compris la clarté des rôles, la profondeur des compétences et l'exhaustivité des outils. Le cadre démontre son efficacité par plus de 330 profils d'agents éducatifs desservant les sujets K-12 avec plus de 1 100 modules de compétences.

de.title: Skalierungsgesetze für pädagogische KI-Agenten
de.summary: Forscher schlagen vor, dass die Leistung von Bildungs-KI-Agenten durch strukturierte Dimensionen über die Modellgröße hinaus skaliert, einschließlich Rollenschärfe, Fähigkeitstiefe und Werkzeugvollständigkeit. Das Framework zeigt Wirksamkeit durch 330+ Bildungsagenten-Profile für K-12-Fächer mit 1.100+ Fähigkeitsmodulen.

es.title: Leyes de Escalado para Agentes de IA Educativa
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen que el rendimiento del agente de IA educativa se escala a través de dimensiones estructuradas más allá del tamaño del modelo, incluida la claridad del rol, la profundidad de la habilidad y la completitud de la herramienta. El marco demuestra su efectividad a través de más de 330 perfiles de agentes educativos que sirven a temas K-12 con más de 1,100 módulos de habilidades.

title: When OpenClaw Meets Hospital: Toward an Agentic Operating System for Dynamic Clinical Workflows
summary: An architecture enables safe deployment of LLM agents in hospitals by restricting execution, implementing document-centric interaction, and managing long-term clinical memory through specialized skill libraries. The system establishes an agentic operating system that maintains safety, transparency, and auditability in clinical workflows.
content:
arXiv:2603.11721v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents extend conventional generative models by integrating reasoning, tool invocation, and persistent memory. Recent studies suggest that such agents may significantly improve clinical workflows by automating documentation, coordinating care processes, and assisting medical decision making. However, despite rapid progress, deploying autonomous agents in healthcare environments remains difficult due to reliability limitations, security risks, and insufficient long-term memory mechanisms. This work proposes an architecture that adapts LLM agents for hospital environments. The design introduces four core components: a restricted execution environment inspired by Linux multi-user systems, a document-centric interaction paradigm connecting patient and clinician agents, a page-indexed memory architecture designed for long-term clinical context management, and a curated medical skills library enabling ad-hoc composition of clinical task sequences. Rather than granting agents unrestricted system access, the architecture constrains actions through predefined skill interfaces and resource isolation. We argue that such a system forms the basis of an Agentic Operating System for Hospital, a computing layer capable of coordinating clinical workflows while maintaining safety, transparency, and auditability. This work grounds the design in OpenClaw, an open-source autonomous agent framework that structures agent capabilities as a curated library of discrete skills, and extends it with the infrastructure-level constraints required for safe clinical deployment.

zh.title: 当OpenClaw遇见医院：走向用于动态临床工作流程的智能体操作系统
zh.summary: 一个架构通过限制执行、实现文档中心交互和通过专门的技能库管理长期临床记忆，实现了LLM代理在医院的安全部署。该系统建立了一个智能体操作系统，在临床工作流程中维持安全性、透明性和可审计性。

fr.title: Quand OpenClaw Rencontre l'Hôpital: Vers un Système d'Exploitation Agentique pour les Flux de Travail Cliniques Dynamiques
fr.summary: Une architecture permet le déploiement sûr des agents LLM dans les hôpitaux en restreignant l'exécution, en mettant en œuvre l'interaction centrée sur les documents et en gérant la mémoire clinique à long terme via des bibliothèques de compétences spécialisées. Le système établit un système d'exploitation agentique qui maintient la sécurité, la transparence et l'auditabilité dans les flux de travail cliniques.

de.title: Wenn OpenClaw auf das Krankenhaus Trifft: Auf dem Weg zu einem Agentensystem für dynamische klinische Arbeitsabläufe
de.summary: Eine Architektur ermöglicht die sichere Bereitstellung von LLM-Agenten in Krankenhäusern durch Ausführungsbeschränkung, Implementierung dokumentzentrierter Interaktion und Verwaltung des langfristigen klinischen Gedächtnisses durch spezialisierte Fähigkeitsbibliotheken. Das System etabliert ein Agentensystem, das Sicherheit, Transparenz und Nachverfolgbarkeit in klinischen Arbeitsabläufen aufrechterhält.

es.title: Cuando OpenClaw se Encuentra con el Hospital: Hacia un Sistema Operativo Agentivo para Flujos de Trabajo Clínicos Dinámicos
es.summary: Una arquitectura permite el despliegue seguro de agentes LLM en hospitales mediante la restricción de ejecución, la implementación de interacción centrada en documentos y la gestión de la memoria clínica a largo plazo a través de bibliotecas de habilidades especializadas. El sistema establece un sistema operativo agentivo que mantiene la seguridad, la transparencia y la auditabilidad en los flujos de trabajo clínicos.

title: Gender Bias in Generative AI-assisted Recruitment Processes
summary: A study of GPT-5's recruitment suggestions reveals gendered linguistic patterns, with the model associating women with emotional traits and men with strategic capabilities despite balanced candidate profiles. The research highlights ethical concerns about bias reproduction in AI-assisted hiring processes.
content:
arXiv:2603.11736v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In recent years, generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) systems have assumed increasingly crucial roles in selection processes, personnel recruitment and analysis of candidates' profiles. However, the employment of large language models (LLMs) risks reproducing, and in some cases amplifying, gender stereotypes and bias already present in the labour market. The objective of this paper is to evaluate and measure this phenomenon, analysing how a state-of-the-art generative model (GPT-5) suggests occupations based on gender and work experience background, focusing on under-35-year-old Italian graduates. The model has been prompted to suggest jobs to 24 simulated candidate profiles, which are balanced in terms of gender, age, experience and professional field. Although no significant differences emerged in job titles and industry, gendered linguistic patterns emerged in the adjectives attributed to female and male candidates, indicating a tendency of the model to associate women with emotional and empathetic traits, while men with strategic and analytical ones. The research raises an ethical question regarding the use of these models in sensitive processes, highlighting the need for transparency and fairness in future digital labour markets.

zh.title: 生成式AI在招聘流程中的性别偏见
zh.summary: 对GPT-5招聘建议的研究揭示了带性别色彩的语言模式，该模型尽管候选人资料平衡，仍将女性与情感特征联系起来，将男性与战略能力联系起来。该研究强调了AI辅助招聘流程中偏见再现的伦理问题。

fr.title: Biais de genre dans les processus de recrutement assistés par l'IA générative
fr.summary: Une étude des suggestions de recrutement de GPT-5 révèle des modèles linguistiques sexistes, avec le modèle associant les femmes à des traits émotionnels et les hommes à des capacités stratégiques malgré des profils de candidats équilibrés. La recherche met en évidence les préoccupations éthiques concernant la reproduction des biais dans les processus de recrutement assistés par l'IA.

de.title: Geschlechtsbias in generativen KI-gestützten Rekrutierungsprozessen
de.summary: Eine Studie zu GPT-5s Rekrutierungsvorschlägen offenbart geschlechtsspezifische sprachliche Muster, wobei das Modell Frauen mit emotionalen Merkmalen und Männer mit strategischen Fähigkeiten assoziiert, obwohl die Kandidatenprofile ausgeglichen sind. Die Forschung hebt ethische Bedenken bezüglich der Reproduktion von Voreingenommenheit in KI-gestützten Einstellungsprozessen hervor.

es.title: Sesgo de género en procesos de reclutamiento asistidos por IA generativa
es.summary: Un estudio de las sugerencias de reclutamiento de GPT-5 revela patrones lingüísticos de género, con el modelo asociando a las mujeres con rasgos emocionales y a los hombres con capacidades estratégicas a pesar de perfiles de candidatos equilibrados. La investigación destaca preocupaciones éticas sobre la reproducción de sesgos en procesos de contratación asistidos por IA.

title: CINDI: Conditional Imputation and Noisy Data Integrity with Flows in Power Grid Data
summary: CINDI is an unsupervised framework using conditional normalizing flows to unify anomaly detection and imputation in noisy multivariate time-series data. The approach preserves the underlying physical and statistical properties of critical infrastructure systems like power grids.
content:
arXiv:2603.11745v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world multivariate time series, particularly in critical infrastructure such as electrical power grids, are often corrupted by noise and anomalies that degrade the performance of downstream tasks. Standard data cleaning approaches often rely on disjoint strategies, which involve detecting errors with one model and imputing them with another. Such approaches can fail to capture the full joint distribution of the data and ignore prediction uncertainty. This work introduces Conditional Imputation and Noisy Data Integrity (CINDI), an unsupervised probabilistic framework designed to restore data integrity in complex time series. Unlike fragmented approaches, CINDI unifies anomaly detection and imputation into a single end-to-end system built on conditional normalizing flows. By modeling the exact conditional likelihood of the data, the framework identifies low-probability segments and iteratively samples statistically consistent replacements. This allows CINDI to efficiently reuse learned information while preserving the underlying physical and statistical properties of the system. We evaluate the framework using real-world grid loss data from a Norwegian power distribution operator, though the methodology is designed to generalize to any multivariate time series domain. The results demonstrate that CINDI yields robust performance compared to competitive baselines, offering a scalable solution for maintaining reliability in noisy environments.

zh.title: CINDI：电网数据中的条件插补和有噪声数据完整性流
zh.summary: CINDI是一个无监督框架，使用条件归一化流来统一有噪声多变量时间序列数据中的异常检测和插补。该方法保留了电网等关键基础设施系统的基础物理和统计特性。

fr.title: CINDI : Imputation Conditionnelle et Intégrité des Données Bruitées avec Flux dans les Données du Réseau Électrique
fr.summary: CINDI est un framework non supervisé utilisant des flux de normalisation conditionnels pour unifier la détection d'anomalies et l'imputation dans les données de séries temporelles multivariées bruitées. L'approche préserve les propriétés physiques et statistiques sous-jacentes des systèmes d'infrastructure critiques comme les réseaux électriques.

de.title: CINDI: Bedingte Imputation und Datenintegrität mit verrauschten Daten in Stromnetzdaten
de.summary: CINDI ist ein unbeaufsichtigtes Framework, das bedingte Normalisierungsflüsse verwendet, um Anomalieerkennung und Imputation in verrauschten multivariaten Zeitreihendaten zu vereinheitlichen. Der Ansatz bewahrt die zugrunde liegenden physikalischen und statistischen Eigenschaften kritischer Infrastruktursysteme wie Stromnetze.

es.title: CINDI: Imputación Condicional e Integridad de Datos Ruidosos con Flujos en Datos de la Red Eléctrica
es.summary: CINDI es un marco no supervisado que utiliza flujos de normalización condicionales para unificar la detección de anomalías e imputación en datos de series temporales multivariadas ruidosas. El enfoque preserva las propiedades físicas y estadísticas subyacentes de sistemas de infraestructura crítica como redes eléctricas.

title: Anomaly detection in time-series via inductive biases in the latent space of conditional normalizing flows
summary: This method detects anomalies in time-series data by relocating the definition of anomaly to a latent space constrained by temporal dynamics, addressing limitations of likelihood-based approaches. The framework provides interpretable diagnostics and reliably detects frequency, amplitude, and noise anomalies.
content:
arXiv:2603.11756v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Deep generative models for anomaly detection in multivariate time-series are typically trained by maximizing data likelihood. However, likelihood in observation space measures marginal density rather than conformity to structured temporal dynamics, and therefore can assign high probability to anomalous or out-of-distribution samples. We address this structural limitation by relocating the notion of anomaly to a prescribed latent space. We introduce explicit inductive biases in conditional normalizing flows, modeling time-series observations within a discrete-time state-space framework that constrains latent representations to evolve according to prescribed temporal dynamics. Under this formulation, expected behavior corresponds to compliance with a specified distribution over latent trajectories, while anomalies are defined as violations of these dynamics. Anomaly detection is consequently reduced to a statistically grounded compliance test, such that observations are mapped to latent space and evaluated via goodness-of-fit tests against the prescribed latent evolution. This yields a principled decision rule that remains effective even in regions of high observation likelihood. Experiments on synthetic and real-world time-series demonstrate reliable detection of anomalies in frequency, amplitude, and observation noise, while providing interpretable diagnostics of model compliance.

zh.title: 通过条件归一化流的潜在空间中的归纳偏置进行时间序列异常检测
zh.summary: 该方法通过将异常定义重新定位到受时间动态约束的潜在空间来检测时间序列数据中的异常，解决了基于似然方法的局限性。该框架提供了可解释的诊断，并可靠地检测频率、振幅和噪声异常。

fr.title: Détection d'anomalies dans les séries temporelles via les biais inductifs dans l'espace latent des flux de normalisation conditionnels
fr.summary: Cette méthode détecte les anomalies dans les données de séries temporelles en relocalisant la définition d'anomalie dans un espace latent contraint par les dynamiques temporelles, résolvant les limitations des approches basées sur la vraisemblance. Le framework fournit des diagnostics interprétables et détecte de manière fiable les anomalies de fréquence, d'amplitude et de bruit.

de.title: Anomalieerkennung in Zeitreihen durch induktive Voreingenommenheiten im latenten Raum bedingter Normalisierungsflüsse
de.summary: Diese Methode erkennt Anomalien in Zeitreihendaten, indem die Definition von Anomalie in einen durch zeitliche Dynamik eingeschränkten latenten Raum verlagert wird, was Einschränkungen likelihood-basierter Ansätze behebt. Das Framework bietet interpretierbare Diagnosen und erkennt zuverlässig Frequenz-, Amplituden- und Rauschanomalien.

es.title: Detección de anomalías en series temporales a través de sesgos inductivos en el espacio latente de flujos de normalización condicionales
es.summary: Este método detecta anomalías en datos de series temporales reubicando la definición de anomalía en un espacio latente restringido por dinámicas temporales, abordando limitaciones de los enfoques basados en probabilidad. El marco proporciona diagnósticos interpretables y detecta de manera confiable anomalías de frecuencia, amplitud y ruido.

title: Understanding Wikidata Qualifiers: An Analysis and Taxonomy
summary: Researchers develop a taxonomy of Wikidata qualifiers by analyzing their usage and importance, categorizing the top 300 into contextual, epistemic, structural, and additional types. The taxonomy guides contributors, improves querying, and enhances knowledge graph design methodologies.
content:
arXiv:2603.11767v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This paper presents an in-depth analysis of Wikidata qualifiers, focusing on their semantics and actual usage, with the aim of developing a taxonomy that addresses the challenges of selecting appropriate qualifiers, querying the graph, and making logical inferences. The study evaluates qualifier importance based on frequency and diversity, using a modified Shannon entropy index to account for the "long tail" phenomenon. By analyzing a Wikidata dump, the top 300 qualifiers were selected and categorized into a refined taxonomy that includes contextual, epistemic/uncertainty, structural, and additional qualifiers. The taxonomy aims to guide contributors in creating and querying statements, improve qualifier recommendation systems, and enhance knowledge graph design methodologies. The results show that the taxonomy effectively covers the most important qualifiers and provides a structured approach to understanding and utilizing qualifiers in Wikidata.

zh.title: 理解维基数据修饰符：分析与分类法
zh.summary: 研究人员通过分析维基数据修饰符的使用情况和重要性来开发其分类法，将前300个分为上下文、认识论、结构和附加类型。该分类法指导贡献者、改进查询，并增强知识图设计方法。

fr.title: Comprendre les qualificateurs de Wikidata : une analyse et une taxonomie
fr.summary: Les chercheurs développent une taxonomie des qualificateurs de Wikidata en analysant leur utilisation et leur importance, en catégorisant les 300 meilleurs en types contextuels, épistémiques, structurels et supplémentaires. La taxonomie guide les contributeurs, améliore les requêtes et améliore les méthodologies de conception de graphes de connaissances.

de.title: Wikidata-Qualifizierer verstehen: Eine Analyse und Taxonomie
de.summary: Forscher entwickeln eine Taxonomie der Wikidata-Qualifizierer durch Analyse ihrer Verwendung und Bedeutung und kategorisieren die Top 300 in kontextuelle, epistemische, strukturelle und zusätzliche Typen. Die Taxonomie leitet Mitwirkende an, verbessert Abfragen und verbessert die Designmethoden für Wissensgraphen.

es.title: Entendiendo los Calificadores de Wikidata: Un Análisis y Taxonomía
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollan una taxonomía de calificadores de Wikidata analizando su uso e importancia, categorizando los 300 principales en tipos contextuales, epistemológicos, estructurales y adicionales. La taxonomía guía a los colaboradores, mejora las consultas y mejora las metodologías de diseño de gráficos de conocimiento.

title: Governing Evolving Memory in LLM Agents: Risks, Mechanisms, and the Stability and Safety Governed Memory (SSGM) Framework
summary: The SSGM framework addresses emergent risks in LLM agent memory systems including memory corruption, semantic drift, and privacy vulnerabilities through consistency verification and dynamic access control. The work establishes governance mechanisms for deploying safe, persistent, and reliable agentic memory systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.11768v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Long-term memory has emerged as a foundational component of autonomous Large Language Model (LLM) agents, enabling continuous adaptation, lifelong multimodal learning, and sophisticated reasoning. However, as memory systems transition from static retrieval databases to dynamic, agentic mechanisms, critical concerns regarding memory governance, semantic drift, and privacy vulnerabilities have surfaced. While recent surveys have focused extensively on memory retrieval efficiency, they largely overlook the emergent risks of memory corruption in highly dynamic environments. To address these emerging challenges, we propose the Stability and Safety-Governed Memory (SSGM) framework, a conceptual governance architecture. SSGM decouples memory evolution from execution by enforcing consistency verification, temporal decay modeling, and dynamic access control prior to any memory consolidation. Through formal analysis and architectural decomposition, we show how SSGM can mitigate topology-induced knowledge leakage where sensitive contexts are solidified into long-term storage, and help prevent semantic drift where knowledge degrades through iterative summarization. Ultimately, this work provides a comprehensive taxonomy of memory corruption risks and establishes a robust governance paradigm for deploying safe, persistent, and reliable agentic memory systems.

zh.title: 治理LLM代理中的演化记忆：风险、机制和稳定性与安全治理记忆（SSGM）框架
zh.summary: SSGM框架通过一致性验证和动态访问控制来解决LLM代理记忆系统中的新兴风险，包括内存损坏、语义漂移和隐私漏洞。该工作建立了治理机制，用于部署安全、持久和可靠的代理记忆系统。

fr.title: Gouverner la mémoire évolutive dans les agents LLM : risques, mécanismes et le cadre Stability and Safety Governed Memory (SSGM)
fr.summary: Le cadre SSGM aborde les risques émergents dans les systèmes de mémoire des agents LLM, notamment la corruption de la mémoire, la dérive sémantique et les vulnérabilités de confidentialité, par la vérification de la cohérence et le contrôle d'accès dynamique. Le travail établit des mécanismes de gouvernance pour déployer des systèmes de mémoire des agents sûrs, persistants et fiables.

de.title: Regulierung des sich entwickelnden Gedächtnisses in LLM-Agenten: Risiken, Mechanismen und das Stability and Safety Governed Memory (SSGM)-Framework
de.summary: Das SSGM-Framework befasst sich mit entstehenden Risiken in LLM-Agent-Speichersystemen, einschließlich Speicherbeschädigungen, semantischem Drift und Datenschutzlücken, durch Konsistenzverifizierung und dynamische Zugriffskontrolle. Die Arbeit etabliert Governance-Mechanismen für die Bereitstellung sicherer, persistenter und zuverlässiger Agentengedächtnissysteme.

es.title: Gobernar la Memoria en Evolución en Agentes LLM: Riesgos, Mecanismos y el Marco de Memoria Regulada para Estabilidad y Seguridad (SSGM)
es.summary: El marco SSGM aborda los riesgos emergentes en los sistemas de memoria de agentes LLM, incluida la corrupción de memoria, la deriva semántica y las vulnerabilidades de privacidad, mediante verificación de consistencia y control de acceso dinámico. El trabajo establece mecanismos de gobernanza para desplegar sistemas de memoria de agentes seguros, persistentes y confiables.

title: An Automatic Text Classification Method Based on Hierarchical Taxonomies, Neural Networks and Document Embedding: The NETHIC Tool
summary: NETHIC is a software tool that combines hierarchical taxonomies, neural networks, and document embedding for automatic text classification with proven effectiveness on both generic and domain-specific corpora. The approach demonstrates significant improvements in performance through integrated document embedding mechanisms.
content:
arXiv:2603.11770v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: This work describes an automatic text classification method implemented in a software tool called NETHIC, which takes advantage of the inner capabilities of highly-scalable neural networks combined with the expressiveness of hierarchical taxonomies. As such, NETHIC succeeds in bringing about a mechanism for text classification that proves to be significantly effective as well as efficient. The tool had undergone an experimentation process against both a generic and a domain-specific corpus, outputting promising results. On the basis of this experimentation, NETHIC has been now further refined and extended by adding a document embedding mechanism, which has shown improvements in terms of performance on the individual networks and on the whole hierarchical model.

zh.title: 基于分层分类法、神经网络和文档嵌入的自动文本分类方法：NETHIC工具
zh.summary: NETHIC是一个软件工具，结合分层分类法、神经网络和文档嵌入，用于自动文本分类，在通用和特定领域的语料库上都证明了有效性。该方法通过集成文档嵌入机制显示了性能的显着改进。

fr.title: Une méthode de classification automatique de texte basée sur les taxonomies hiérarchiques, les réseaux de neurones et l'intégration de documents : l'outil NETHIC
fr.summary: NETHIC est un outil logiciel qui combine les taxonomies hiérarchiques, les réseaux de neurones et l'intégration de documents pour la classification automatique de textes avec une efficacité éprouvée sur les corpus génériques et spécifiques au domaine. L'approche démontre des améliorations significatives de performance grâce à des mécanismes d'intégration de documents intégrés.

de.title: Eine automatische Textklassifizierungsmethode basierend auf hierarchischen Taxonomien, neuronalen Netzen und Dokumenteinbettung: Das NETHIC-Tool
de.summary: NETHIC ist ein Softwaretool, das hierarchische Taxonomien, neuronale Netze und Dokumenteinbettung für die automatische Textklassifizierung kombiniert und eine bewiesene Wirksamkeit bei generischen und domänenspezifischen Korpora aufweist. Der Ansatz demonstriert erhebliche Leistungsverbesserungen durch integrierte Dokumenteinbettungsmechanismen.

es.title: Un Método Automático de Clasificación de Texto Basado en Taxonomías Jerárquicas, Redes Neuronales e Incrustación de Documentos: La Herramienta NETHIC
es.summary: NETHIC es una herramienta de software que combina taxonomías jerárquicas, redes neuronales e incrustación de documentos para la clasificación automática de textos con eficacia comprobada en corpus genéricos y específicos de dominio. El enfoque demuestra mejoras significativas de rendimiento a través de mecanismos integrados de incrustación de documentos.
